questions: Were all 7 implementation plan steps executed successfully?
Does the config system preserve existing fields during save operations?
Does hlx --version output the package.json version instead of a hardcoded string?
Does hlx update check GitHub main HEAD SHA and report update status correctly?
Does the auto-update check respect the autoUpdate setting and HLX_SKIP_UPDATE_CHECK guard?
Is the prepare script present for git-based npm install?

answers.answer: Yes, all 7 steps were executed: (1) config system extended with InstallSource type and read-merge-write saveConfig, (2) version.ts created with getPackageVersion, (3) check.ts created with fetchRemoteSha and isUpdateAvailable, (4) perform.ts created with performUpdate, (5) index.ts orchestration created with runUpdate and checkAutoUpdate, (6) CLI entry point wired with update command, auto-update hook, and version fix, (7) prepare script added to package.json.
evidence: All CHK-01 through CHK-09 checks executed

answers.answer: Yes, saveConfig now uses read-merge-write pattern. Tested with apiKey+url config, then enable-auto and disable-auto toggles preserved all existing fields including apiKey, url, and installSource.
evidence: Config after --enable-auto: {apiKey, url, autoUpdate:true} — all fields present
Config after --disable-auto: {apiKey, url, autoUpdate:false} — all fields present

answers.answer: Yes, hlx --version now outputs 1.2.0 (the package.json version) instead of the hardcoded 0.1.0.
Yes, hlx update checks the remote SHA via git ls-remote and compares with local installSource.commit. Reports 'Already up to date.' when SHAs match, performs npm install -g from GitHub when behind.
evidence: First run: 'Installing latest...' + 'Update complete.' with exit 0
Second run (after metadata persisted): 'Already up to date.' with exit 0

answers.answer: Yes, the auto-update check runs only when autoUpdate is true AND installSource is canonical. HLX_SKIP_UPDATE_CHECK=1 prevents the check entirely. Commands in SKIP_AUTO_UPDATE set also bypass it.
evidence: With autoUpdate:false, login command shows no update messages (0.091s)

answers.answer: Yes, package.json scripts now includes 'prepare': 'npm run build'.
evidence: Verified via node -e: scripts.prepare === 'npm run build'

statement: All implementation plan steps for HLX-316 have been executed successfully. The helix-cli now has: (1) a fixed config system with read-merge-write saveConfig and new InstallSource/autoUpdate types, (2) a runtime package version reader replacing the hardcoded 0.1.0, (3) a GitHub SHA check module using git ls-remote, (4) a self-update module using npm install -g from GitHub, (5) an update command handler with --enable-auto/--disable-auto flags and auto-update orchestration, (6) CLI entry point wired with update command case, auto-update pre-command hook, and fixed --version, and (7) a prepare script for git-based npm install. TypeScript typecheck and build pass. All verification checks pass except CHK-05 which is blocked by sandbox git restrictions on direct bash git commands (but works correctly through Node child_process.execSync).
